signals.summary: 68 percent of complaints filed with the contractor went unresolved during the audit period.
75 incidents that risked recipients' health or safety were not investigated or reported to HCPF.
HCPF paid $291,600 for claims that did not comply with federal and state requirements, including claims without proof rides occurred.

signals.entityName: Colorado Department of Health Care Policy and Financing (HCPF)
signals.summary: Approximately $5.2 million in claims were potentially noncompliant, including taxi claims at incorrect rates.

signals.entityName: Colorado Department of Health Care Policy and Financing (HCPF)
signals.summary: 32 acts of potential mistreatment of at-risk adults during NEMT rides were not reported to law enforcement as required.
Department did not ensure contractor complied with requirements for on-time pickups, timely call answering, and complaint resolution.
IntelliRide publicly acknowledged audit findings and stated corrective actions were underway.
Contract requires monthly reporting of trip denials and no-shows — key unmet-demand proxy without PHI if obtained via CORA/contract.
Monthly dialysis report must include missed treatments due to late delivery and provider no-shows — direct missed-care transport signal.

signals.entityName: HCPF NEMT Member Feedback / Complaint Program
signals.summary: Public complaint form includes Driver Issue (No Show/Late/Unsafe) — aggregate complaint data not published but intake exists.

closestPathToFailureMetrics.metricSource: Monthly Performance Report — denials, no-shows, dialysis summary
closestPathToFailureMetrics.rationale: Colorado NEMT broker contract §5.10.2 requires monthly reporting of trips denied, provider no-shows, missed/late dialysis appointments, and complaints per 1,000 trips — the closest contractually mandated failure metrics without ride-level PHI.

closestPathToFailureMetrics.entityName: Colorado Office of the State Auditor
closestPathToFailureMetrics.rationale: OSA audit documents measured failure rates: 68% unresolved complaints, 75 unreported safety incidents, and noncompliant claim payments — evidence-backed baseline of broker stress without PHI.
Contract mandates late-delivery time bands and count of missed dialysis treatments due to transport — direct bottleneck signal for recurring medical transport.

closestPathToFailureMetrics.entityName: HCPF NEMT Member Feedback / Complaint Program
closestPathToFailureMetrics.rationale: Public intake captures no-show, scheduling, and driver safety complaints — obtainable in aggregate via CORA without individual trip records.

closestPathToFailureMetrics.entityName: Colorado Department of Health Care Policy and Financing (HCPF)
closestPathToFailureMetrics.metricSource: CORA request for broker Monthly Performance Reports
closestPathToFailureMetrics.rationale: HCPF receives contractor monthly reports under contract — CORA may yield aggregate denial/no-show/dialysis metrics even when trip-level PHI is redacted.
